title: betula alba var. carpatica
intro: Betula alba var. carpatica, also known as the White Birch, is a tree species native to Europe, characterized by its white bark and slender branches. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate various health issues. The bark of this plant has been found to have antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for heart health and potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases, such as cardiovascular disease. Betula alba var. carpatica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to soothe digestive problems and reduce pain.
